description: Capabilities document advertises supports_idempotency_key=true but omits limits.idempotency_key_ttl_seconds. RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.2 and the schema's allOf/if-then clause (acdp-capabilities.schema.json lines 107–121) require the TTL to be present whenever idempotency support is advertised. Without a declared TTL, consumers cannot compute when a previous Idempotency-Key→response mapping has expired (RFC-ACDP-0003 §6.2).

expected.consumer_action: MUST NOT use Idempotency-Key against this registry (treat the registry as if supports_idempotency_key were false), AND MUST log/surface the inconsistency so operators can correct the capabilities document.
expected.rationale: A registry that advertises idempotency support without a TTL is sending contradictory information. The schema-level enforcement catches the most common misconfiguration (toggling the boolean without populating the TTL field below it).
